trust_transition_candidate_class_identif.summary: Prospective live change in accepted trust-root posture, including introduction, retirement, narrowing, replacement, or supersession of root-bearing anchors.
Prospective live change in verifier-side acceptance posture, strictness, or canonical trust-policy interpretation.
Prospective live change in which signer identities, signature chains, or signing-bearing validity envelopes remain accepted.
Prospective canonical declaration that a signer, root, chain, artifact class, or equivalent trust-bearing path is revoked.
Prospective live carrying-forward of revocation meaning across release selection, trust-bearing visibility, consumer interpretation, and local validation posture.
Prospective bounded high-urgency reduction of trust acceptance posture intended to contain active risk.
Prospective live widening, replacement, or superseding trust posture that must preserve continuity with the previously reduced or revoked state.

prerequisite_class_identifiers.summary: Canonical governance, checkpoint law, and ownership constraints remain satisfied before a live trust-transition candidate is admissible.
The candidate carries explicit safety posture, privilege posture, and human-review posture appropriate to blast radius.
The candidate preserves release identity, release contract profile, release-index and resolution semantics, and target applicability.
The candidate is operator-transaction-bearing, receipt-bearing, and provenance-continuous.
The candidate preserves the difference among archive continuity, mirror visibility, publication posture, and trust truth.
Any runtime-facing trust candidate remains subordinate to lifecycle, replay, snapshot, isolation, coexistence, and hotswap boundary law.
The candidate depends on unadmitted distributed authority, local-only effects, missing continuity, or runtime cutover overclaim and therefore remains outside admission.

gate_receipt_archive_index_runtime_depen: Publication, trust, and licensing gates remain upstream and gate passage does not by itself satisfy live trust-transition admissibility.
Live trust-transition candidates are operator-transaction-bearing and require canonical receipt continuity rather than local logs or wrapper output.
Release identity, release contract profile, release-index and resolution semantics remain upstream truth and must not be silently redefined by trust posture changes.
Archive presence preserves history but does not imply live trust readiness.
Mirror visibility preserves availability posture but does not define trust convergence or revocation propagation truth.
Any runtime-facing trust claim remains subordinate to lifecycle, replay, snapshot, isolation, coexistence, and hotswap doctrine.
Canary, downgrade, rehearsal, and rollback-alignment doctrines may constrain later live trust transitions without constituting operational admission by themselves.

invalidity_failure_categories.summary: Authority ordering, checkpoint posture, or ownership constraints were not explicitly satisfied.
The candidate lacks the required review, privilege, or approval posture.
Release identity, release contract profile, target applicability, or resolution semantics are incomplete or contradictory.
Canonical receipt continuity is missing, partial, contradictory, or non-reconstructable.
The action cannot be linked coherently across declaration, propagation, containment, restoration, or supersession history.
Archive or mirror state was treated as sufficient proof of trust-transition readiness.
Local verifier behavior was misrepresented as canonical revocation propagation.
A revocation declaration exists but its continuity across governed surfaces is missing or undefined.
Publication or mirror visibility movement was treated as trust convergence.
The candidate implicitly claims live cutover, hotswap, or runtime convergence beyond current doctrine.
The candidate depends on distributed authority or quorum convergence that has not yet been constitutionally admitted.
The candidate remains explicitly outside the current admissible maturity band.

canonical: upstream governance, safety, release, trust, runtime, and hotswap doctrine
canonical operator transaction and receipt continuity records
canonical trust policy and trust-root registries with intact provenance

prohibited_prerequisite_shapes: local verifier behavior treated as revocation propagation proof
mirror removal treated as revocation proof
publication visibility treated as trust convergence
mirror state treated as trust truth
archive presence treated as live trust readiness
doctrine existence treated as operational admission
distributed authority planning assuming trust convergence without prerequisite admission
